Title: 2′-Deoxymugineic acid promotes growth of rice (Oryza sativa L.) by orchestrating iron and nitrate uptake processes under high pH conditions

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential micronutrient for chlorophyll formation, photosynthesis, redox enzymes, and growth.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DMA treatment significantly reduced YSL15 expression, especially at high pH, reflecting improved Fe status and distinct sensing of Fe-DMA.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YSL15 transcripts were down-regulated by DMA or EDTA in shoots. 2. In roots at pH 8.0, DMA decreased YSL15 more strongly than EDTA. 3. YSL15 is implicated in Fe-DMA uptake and phloem transport.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Fe deficiency; root basal transcript higher at pH 8.0 than pH 5.8; reduced after DMA treatment.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Fe deficiency; root basal transcript higher at pH 8.0 than pH 5.8; reduced after DMA treatment.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous DMA or EDTA; hydroponic growth at pH 5.8 or 8.0.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; microarray (GSE56455); physiological 55Fe uptake context discussed from prior studies.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower expression under sufficient Fe may limit further Fe-DMA uptake.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Transport target for improving Fe uptake on alkaline soils, especially in combination with DMA biosynthesis traits.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as important for Fe-DMA transport into phloem; YSL15 and/or YSL18 likely mediate efficient Fe-DMA uptake.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports iron chelation and internal transport/homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DMA or EDTA reduced NAS1 expression, with DMA showing stronger repression under high pH, indicating feedback from improved Fe availability.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAS1 was down-regulated by chelator treatment in shoots and roots. 2. DMA caused stronger reduction than EDTA at high pH. 3. NAS1 is part of the iron deficiency response linked to phytosiderophore-mediated uptake.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced under Fe deficiency; decreased after DMA or EDTA treatment.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under Fe deficiency; decreased after DMA or EDTA treatment.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous DMA or EDTA at pH 5.8 or 8.0.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess repression could limit endogenous chelator production.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for tuning endogenous iron chelator biosynthesis to improve Fe efficiency under calcareous conditions.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper conclusion specifically notes nicotianamine synthase expression was down-regulated by DMA under high pH.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports iron chelation and internal transport/homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NAS2 transcripts showed patterns similar to NAS1, decreasing after DMA treatment, consistent with reduced Fe deficiency signaling.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAS2 was analyzed by qRT-PCR. 2. Its transcriptional pattern resembled NAS1. 3. DMA reduced NAS2 expression consistent with improved Fe status.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced under Fe deficiency; decreased after DMA or EDTA treatment.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under Fe deficiency; decreased after DMA or EDTA treatment.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous DMA or EDTA at pH 5.8 or 8.0.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential companion target with NAS1 for engineering Fe homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported to show similar transcriptional patterns to NAS1.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen source for assimilation and plant growth.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DMA and EDTA drastically induced NRT2.1 in roots under both normal and high pH, linking improved Fe status to nitrate uptake.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NRT2.1 was induced approximately 80-fold at pH 5.8 and 100-fold at pH 8.0 by chelator treatments in roots. 2. qRT-PCR confirmed strong induction by DMA and EDTA. 3. Induction is associated with enhanced nitrate reductase activity and growth.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-specific induction by nitrate/chelator treatment; not similarly induced in shoots.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-specific induction by nitrate/chelator treatment; not similarly induced in shoots.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous DMA or EDTA; pH 5.8 or 8.0; hydroponic culture.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target to combine Fe-efficiency and nitrate use efficiency under alkaline conditions.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper repeatedly highlights NRT2.1 as a major nitrate transporter induced by chelator treatment.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports high-affinity nitrate uptake and nitrogen nutrition.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NAR2.1 transcription was induced by DMA and EDTA similarly to NRT2.1, supporting activation of high-affinity nitrate uptake.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAR2.1 showed induction patterns similar to NRT2.1. 2. Up-regulation occurred under both pH 5.8 and pH 8.0. 3. This supports coordinated activation of nitrate uptake machinery by Fe chelator treatment.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in roots after DMA or EDTA treatment.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in roots after DMA or EDTA treatment.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous DMA or EDTA; pH 5.8 or 8.0.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving nitrate uptake when stacked with iron acquisition traits.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts with NRT2 transporters in high-affinity nitrate uptake.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces nitrate to nitrite using NADH/NADPH as electron donor in nitrate assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant nitrogen nutrition and growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DMA or EDTA treatment increased NIA1/2 transcript levels and nitrate reductase activity, supporting enhanced nitrate assimilation under normal and high pH conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NIA1/2 transcripts were up-regulated by DMA and EDTA. 2. Increased nitrate reductase activity in shoots accompanied chelator treatment. 3. The induction supports a link between iron availability and nitrate assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in shoots and roots after DMA or EDTA treatment under pH 5.8 and 8.0.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in shoots and roots after DMA or EDTA treatment under pH 5.8 and 8.0.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous 2′-deoxymugineic acid or EDTA; hydroponic culture at pH 5.8 or pH 8.0.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; nitrate reductase enzyme activity assay; microarray (GSE56455).
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used with improved iron acquisition traits to coordinate nitrate assimilation and growth on alkaline soils.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primers used for NIA1 may also have amplified NIA2 because of high sequence similarity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces nitrate to nitrite using reducing equivalents from NADH/NADPH.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant nitrogen nutrition and growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DMA or EDTA treatment increased NIA1/2 transcript levels and nitrate reductase activity, supporting enhanced nitrate assimilation under normal and high pH conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NIA1/2 transcripts were up-regulated by DMA and EDTA. 2. Increased nitrate reductase activity in shoots accompanied chelator treatment. 3. The induction supports a link between iron availability and nitrate assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in shoots and roots after DMA or EDTA treatment under pH 5.8 and 8.0.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in shoots and roots after DMA or EDTA treatment under pH 5.8 and 8.0.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous 2′-deoxymugineic acid or EDTA; hydroponic culture at pH 5.8 or pH 8.0.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; nitrate reductase enzyme activity assay; microarray (GSE56455).
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target with iron acquisition traits to sustain growth under alkaline conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Measured together with NIA1 in qRT-PCR because of high sequence similarity.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Iron uptake and phytosiderophore-mediated iron homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates YSL15 and NAS1 expression; its own transcript level is reduced by chelator treatment, especially DMA.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential micronutrient for chlorophyll formation, photosynthesis, redox enzymes, and growth.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DMA treatment more strongly reduced IRO2 expression than EDTA, consistent with stronger attenuation of iron deficiency responses.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRO2 transcript levels were considerably decreased by DMA or EDTA. 2. Down-regulation by DMA was stronger than by EDTA. 3. The result supports a distinct Fe-sensing response to Fe-DMA versus Fe-EDTA.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of phytosiderophore-Fe uptake system in rice.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Environmental iron status; chelator form of supplied iron (DMA-Fe versus EDTA-Fe); high pH-induced Fe deficiency.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous DMA or EDTA under pH 5.8 and 8.0.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray (GSE56455); qRT-PCR inference through cited regulatory relationship and expression profiling.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced under Fe deficiency; decreased after DMA or EDTA treatment in shoots and roots.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under Fe deficiency; decreased after DMA or EDTA treatment in shoots and roots.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely feedback repression by improved iron status; not directly tested.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Repression may reduce iron acquisition machinery when Fe is sufficient.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory node for engineering alkali tolerance and iron efficiency, but requires tuning to avoid unnecessary Fe-deficiency signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified in discussion as regulator of YSL15 and NAS1 and a regulator for the phytosiderophore-Fe uptake system.
